what_it_does: Builds a browser page that loads layout and decision data, selects the event-streams decision record, renders it as a composed decision-card slide, and mounts the generated view into the page.
observations: The page defines a minimal HTML shell with a mount element and inline style for full-height display.
It loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts.
It uses shared CSS from /surface/styles/piece.css and /surface/styles/phone.css.
It loads runtime scripts for surface, organisms, decision rendering, and archetype support.
The script fetches /dna/layouts.json and /content/decisions.json in parallel.
It selects the decision whose id is event-streams and renders it with address decision://global/event-streams and pending state.
The rendered view receives the dv-c class before being appended to #mount.
standouts: The file is a thin declarative page wrapper around a generative runtime renderer.
The rendered decision identity is assembled from external content plus local address and state fields.
The comment explicitly frames the page as a composed decision view containing both interface and slide-telling.
conventions: Uses a single page-specific ID constant to specialize a shared decision-card renderer.
Keeps page structure minimal and delegates layout, content, and rendering to external runtime/data files.
Uses inline page-scoped CSS only for shell behavior while relying on shared stylesheets for presentation.
Uses an async IIFE to perform data loading and DOM mounting.
concerns: There is no visible error handling for failed fetches, missing layout data, or a missing decision record.
The file depends on multiple absolute-path assets and remote font loading.
The renderer calls assume the global DNA object and expected runtime functions are available.
notes: This is a file-local entry point that materializes the event-streams decision as a composed decision-card slide.
questions: What should the page display if the event-streams record is not present?
Is pending always the intended state for this decision view?
Should the external font dependency have a local fallback path beyond CSS font fallback?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated composed decision-view purpose matches the implementation: the file constructs a contained decision interface and explanatory slide view through the shared renderer.
intention_signals: It seems intended to create one concrete decision-slide instance from reusable archetype, layout, and content substrates while keeping this file only responsible for selecting and mounting the event-streams record.
novelty: The page treats a decision card as both an interface object and a slide object, composed through runtime archetype rendering rather than hand-authored markup.
contribution: Provides a concrete HTML substrate for mounting an addressed event-streams decision-card slide, preserving the pattern of file-local specialization over shared layout, content, and rendering assets.
summary_for_embedding: A minimal HTML entry point that loads shared decision-card runtime assets, fetches layout and decision data, selects the event-streams record, renders a pending composed decision slide, and mounts it into the page.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for expressing the event-streams decision as a generated, addressable, composed decision-card view within the counterpart-design surface system.
